mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.5 percent, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts household income and consumption expectations, driving positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 8.5, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in industrial production and housing starts indicate broad-based economic expansion, which lifts corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline INDPRO-only or HOUST-only signal in out-of-sample testing, or forward returns are flat when both series rise concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Stable consumer price inflation anchors real interest rates and prevents Federal Reserve policy tightening, allowing equity valuations to expand and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL remains within a 0.5 percent monthly band, or the signal exhibits a negative Rank IC during periods of actual Fed tightening.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in commercial and industrial lending activity restricts corporate capital expenditure and working capital, triggering negative earnings revisions and driving down S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 50% of triggered credit contraction episodes, or RSAFS declines show no correlation with subsequent equity drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production capacity utilization reflects improving supply-side efficiency and lower unit labor costs, which expands corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly higher when INDPRO rises month-over-month, or the signal produces negative returns during supply chain disruptions.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent job growth acceleration and unemployment rate declines confirm a self-reinforcing labor market recovery, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive quarterly returns when both series move favorably, or the interaction term shows zero statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating real GDP growth signals a transition from expansion to contraction, prompting downward revisions to corporate earnings estimates and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or flat when GDPC1 growth decelerates, or the signal produces false negatives during normal business cycle fluctuations.
